key_insight: Raising cold-spray pressure from 30 to 60 bar increases copper particle velocity past the bonding threshold, driving a stronger, more ductile, and better-conducting deposit.
engineering_relevance: This identifies spray pressure as a primary control knob for tuning the process–structure–property balance in copper CSAM parts where both electrical conductivity and mechanical integrity matter.
method: Cold-sprayed copper on a LightSPEE3D system at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar.
Measured particle velocity, XRD crystallite/dislocation changes, hardness, conductivity, tensile behavior, and SEM fracture/microstructure.
Compared property trends to infer pressure-driven bonding and recrystallisation mechanisms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports pressure-dependent improvements in conductivity and ductility, with XRD indicating crystallite refinement/dislocation changes and tensile tests showing a transition toward fully ductile fracture at higher pressures.
watch_item: Higher pressure may improve bonding but also promotes recovery/recrystallisation and hardness loss, so the best operating window must balance strength, ductility, and conductivity for the target application.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
